name: Parry
classification_notes: Parry is explicitly rooted in Victorian slab serif tradition, featuring bracketed or geometric serifs and a wide weight range. Its softer, rounder forms distinguish it from rigid nineteenth-century models while remaining squarely within the slab serif genre.
summary: A contemporary slab serif by Artur Schmal that softens Victorian slab traditions into a rounder, more approachable form across eight weights.
description: Designed by Artur Schmal, Parry takes the sturdy slab serifs of the Victorian era as its starting point but departs from their rigidity with rounder, softer letterforms. Sixteen styles span eight weights from UltraThin to ExtraBlack, each with a matching italic, giving the family considerable range. The foundry positions it across corporate identity, book typography, magazines and large-format advertising.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe Parry as rooted in the sturdy slab serifs common to the Victorian age, yet unmistakably contemporary in its rounder, softer image. They note it has proven versatile since release, serving everything from corporate identities and book typography to magazines and billboard-scale advertising campaigns.

name: Parry Condensed
classification_notes: Rooted in Victorian-era slab serifs but drawn with a rounder, softer quality that distinguishes it from the rigid slab forms of the nineteenth century. The condensed cut retains the slab serif structure across eight weights with matching italics.
summary: A condensed slab serif by Artur Schmal that takes Victorian-era slab forms and softens them into something rounder and more adaptable across eight weights.
description: Where Victorian slab serifs tend toward rigidity, Parry Condensed rounds off the edges to produce a warmer, more flexible cut. Eight weights from UltraThin to ExtraBlack are paired with a full set of italics, giving the family breadth for everything from text-weight body copy to heavy display settings. The condensed proportions suit editorial layouts, advertising and corporate identity work where space is at a premium.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe Parry Condensed as rooted in the sturdy slab serifs of the Victorian age, but unmistakably contemporary in character. It produces a rounder and softer image than the often rigid slab serifs of that period, and has proven versatile across corporate identities, book typography, magazines and billboard advertising. The family comprises 8 roman and 8 italic styles, available for desktop, web and app licensing.

name: Parry Grotesque
classification_notes: Rooted in the quirky Victorian-era grotesques, Parry Grotesque is a sans-serif companion to the serif Parry family, with the irregular details and personality characteristic of nineteenth-century grotesque types redrawn for contemporary use.
summary: A sans-serif companion to the Parry family, drawing on Victorian grotesque quirks and recast for contemporary use across eight weights with matching italics.
description: Designed by Artur Schmal as the sans-serif counterpart to his Parry serif, this typeface takes its cues from the idiosyncratic grotesques of the nineteenth century while remaining clearly contemporary in execution. Sixteen styles span UltraThin through ExtraBlack with full italic coverage, and a condensed subfamily extends the range further. The family handles corporate identity, book typography, magazines and large-format advertising with equal ease.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe Parry Grotesque as rooted in the quirky grotesques common to the Victorian age, yet unmistakably contemporary in character. A sans-serif companion to Parry, it has proven versatile across applications ranging from corporate identities and book typography to magazines and billboard-scale advertising campaigns. Available in 8 roman and 8 italic styles for desktop, web and app licensing.

name: Parry Grotesque Condensed
classification_notes: Described as rooted in Victorian-era quirky grotesques, this is a condensed sans-serif companion to Parry, fitting squarely within the grotesque tradition despite its contemporary execution.
summary: A condensed grotesque by Artur Schmal drawing on Victorian quirk while remaining firmly contemporary, offered in eight weights with matching italics.
description: The condensed cut of Parry Grotesque, designed by Artur Schmal as a sans-serif companion to the Parry serif family. Sixteen styles span UltraThin to ExtraBlack with full italic coverage, making it serviceable across corporate identity, magazine work and large-format advertising. Its Victorian DNA lends a mild eccentricity that keeps it from being entirely neutral.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe Parry Grotesque Condensed as rooted in the quirky grotesques of the Victorian era, while remaining unmistakably contemporary. Designed by Artur Schmal, it is the sans-serif companion to Parry, praised for its versatility across applications ranging from corporate identities and book typography to magazines and billboard-sized campaigns.

name: Bonnie SemiCondensed
classification_notes: Bonnie draws on twentieth-century geometric sans traditions with soft, humanist detailing; the SemiCondensed is one of three width variants of the same design.
summary: A semi-condensed geometric sans with soft curves and a large x-height, designed by Artur Schmal as part of a three-width family for versatile copy-fitting.
description: Bonnie SemiCondensed sits between the standard and condensed widths of Artur Schmal's Bonnie family, spanning nine weights from UltraThin to Black with matching italics. The design borrows the structural clarity of mid-century geometric sans-serifs while introducing rounded details and an open construction that keep it approachable at text sizes. Three widths across the full Bonnie suite make it a practical choice where column widths vary or space is tight.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe Bonnie as a sans serif combining the bold qualities of twentieth-century geometric typefaces with soft, human details that create a friendly atmosphere. Its capitals are described as powerful and clear, while the lowercase features a large x-height, open construction and curvy details that balance aesthetics with functionality. Three widths make it a tool for copy-fitting across a variety of layouts.

name: Bonnie
classification_notes: Bonnie draws from twentieth-century geometric sans traditions while incorporating open, humanist details and a large x-height, placing it at the junction of geometric and humanist sans-serif.
summary: A geometric sans by Artur Schmal that blends the bold presence of mid-century geometric typefaces with open, curvy details intended to produce a warmer, more approachable tone.
description: Available across three widths, Bonnie is designed for copy-fitting flexibility across a range of layouts. The capitals carry clarity and weight while the lowercase, with its large x-height and open construction, keeps the family legible and approachable at text sizes. Nine weights from UltraThin to Black, each with a matching italic, extend across the standard, SemiCondensed and Condensed variants for a total of 54 styles in the full suite.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe Bonnie as a clean sans serif that combines the bold geometric qualities of twentieth-century typefaces with soft, human details that create a friendly atmosphere. The capitals speak a powerful, clear language while the lowercase, with its large x-height and curvy construction, balances aesthetics with functionality. Three widths make it a practical tool for copy-fitting across varied layouts.

name: Parry Editorial Design Packs
classification_notes: The packs centre on Parry and Parry Grotesque, which are grotesque and humanist sans families, alongside Bonnie, a serif. The overall character of the collection skews grotesque sans-led.
summary: Cross-family licensing bundles by Artur Schmal drawing on Parry, Parry Grotesque and Bonnie for editorial typographic systems.
description: Three tiered packs pulling styles from Parry, Parry Grotesque, Parry Condensed, Parry Grotesque Condensed, Bonnie and its condensed variants into a single licence. The Basic pack covers 12 styles, the Extensive 28, and the Parry Bonnie pack 35, each designed to give editorial projects a coherent palette without licensing each family separately. Language support spans a wide range of Latin-script languages, and styles include italics and condensed cuts suited to complex editorial layouts.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe these packs as compiled to meet the special typographic needs of editorial projects such as scientific papers, newspapers, magazines and annual reports, which often require fonts from several families. Each pack is priced to offer strong value and is available for desktop, web and app licensing.

name: Bonnie Condensed
classification_notes: Bonnie is rooted in twentieth-century geometric sans traditions but softened with humanist details and a large x-height; the Condensed cut retains these qualities in a narrower width.
summary: A condensed geometric sans with humanist softening, offering nine weights and matching italics for space-efficient typesetting.
description: The Condensed cut of Artur Schmal's Bonnie family carries the same blend of geometric structure and rounded, open detailing as its wider siblings. Nine weights from UltraThin to Black, each with a matching italic, give the family considerable range across display and text sizes. Its large x-height and open construction keep it legible at smaller sizes, making it a practical choice for editorial and layout work where horizontal space is tight.
foundry_blurb: Original Type describe Bonnie as a sans serif that combines the bold qualities of twentieth-century geometric typefaces with soft, human details that create a friendly atmosphere. The capitals are powerful and clear, while the lowercase characters feature a large x-height, open construction and curvy details balancing aesthetics with functionality. Three widths, including Condensed, make it a versatile tool for copy fitting across varied layouts.
